c2_classification_correction.reason: The rolling-window exact retention model IS proven (c2_retention_model.json: ROLLING_WINDOW_EXACT_MODEL_PROVEN, peak 15 frames / 93312000 bytes). The rejection reason is performance failure: candidate is slower than reference in all three paired runs (directional_stability=false, median_of_paired_differences=-46.82s). The resource model is measured and bounded; the failure is performance, not resources.

canonical_signature_provenance.root_cause: CODE_CHANGE_NOT_ENVIRONMENT_ANOMALY. The original benchmark at HEAD acb424f904bc4b3459f6ad2ceb9f8c701cedb69b did NOT access stream.codec_context in pyav_iter_frames. The current code (since the evidence-observer infrastructure was added) DOES access stream.codec_context. This access changes PyAV 18.0.0 / NVDEC HW decode initialization, producing systematically different pixel values for the same input bitstream. Different pixels lead to different feature extraction, different change detection, different segments, and therefore a different canonical signature.
canonical_signature_provenance.implication: The 8cc06c6a signature is valid ONLY for code that does NOT access stream.codec_context in the decode loop. The 5a7c4538 signature is valid for the current code that DOES access it. The signature mismatch is a direct consequence of production code change, not an environment or clip anomaly. This provenance gap blocks the exact-semantics gate until either (a) the codec_context access is removed from the hot decode path, or (b) the immutable signature is re-baselined under the current code.
